title: phelipanche zangezuri
intro:
Phelipanche zangezuri, also known as Zangezuri broomrape, is a parasitic plant species that obtains its nutrients by attaching to the roots of other plants, specifically those belonging to the Poaceae family.

Phelipanche zangezuri is reported to possess medicinal properties due to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments.

The health benefits associated with Phelipanche zangezuri include its potential to reduce inflammation, exhibit antimicrobial properties, and possibly even exhibit anticancer activities, although further research is required to confirm these effects.

In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling the roots in water, and it has been used to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
